description_long: This is a medieval-style poleaxe featuring a long, straight wooden handle with a dark finish and a robust, multi-functional metal head. The head consists of a central pointed spike, a broad axe blade on one side, and a hammer or flanged beak on the opposite side, all rendered in a dark metallic color. The handle appears cylindrical and smooth, with a metal ring or guard near the head for added grip and protection. The overall construction suggests durability and effectiveness for both cutting and piercing tasks. The axe head is securely mounted to the handle, likely using a traditional wedged or pinned method. This tool would be suitable for display, reenactment, or as a prop in urban or historical-themed environments.
description: A medieval-style poleaxe with a long wooden handle and a dark, multi-edged metal head featuring a spike, axe blade, and hammer.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full profile of the axe head, including the broad blade, central spike, and hammer side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the axe head and the pointed spike extending forward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the axe head, with the hammer or beak side and the handle's alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the thin edge of the blade and the pointed spike.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth finish and a metal guard near the head.
attribute_blade_design: Multi-edged head with a broad axe blade, central spike, and hammer/beak; symmetrical and functional with no visible decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged or pinned mounting securing the metal head to the wooden handle.
